raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need find files under /testbed that have "FooBar" in path. Use find /testbed -type f -path '*FooBar*' -exec cp -p {} . \; but preserve mode, ownership, timestamp: cp -p preserves mode, ownership? -p preserves mode, ownership, timestamps. But cp -p may not preserve ownership unless run as root. Use cp -a? cp -a preserves everything. But -a includes directories. Use cp -a --preserve=mode,ownership,timestamps? Actually cp -a is same as -dR --preserve=all. So cp -a {} .? But cp -a will copy directories too. We just want files. Use cp -p. So command: find /testbed -type f -path '*FooBar*' -exec cp -p {} . \;
raw_output_payload.reasoning_content: We need find files under /testbed that have "FooBar" in path. Use find /testbed -type f -path '*FooBar*' -exec cp -p {} . \; but preserve mode, ownership, timestamp: cp -p preserves mode, ownership? -p preserves mode, ownership, timestamps. But cp -p may not preserve ownership unless run as root. Use cp -a? cp -a preserves everything. But -a includes directories. Use cp -a --preserve=mode,ownership,timestamps? Actually cp -a is same as -dR --preserve=all. So cp -a {} .? But cp -a will copy directories too. We just want files. Use cp -p. So command: find /testbed -type f -path '*FooBar*' -exec cp -p {} . \;
